patterns.name: close
patterns.role: A round, red close button for dialogs/drawers/dismissible panels; designed to be the most explicit "go away" affordance

patterns.name: readable
patterns.role: Constrain content to a comfortable reading width (~900px) without imposing a hard column; place on long-form text wrappers

patterns.name: narrow
patterns.role: Tighter content width (~50ch) for forms, dialogs, and single-column UI; configurable via `--narrow-width`

patterns.name: flex
patterns.role: Flex row primitive with gap honoring `--gap`; reach for it when a one-line cluster of items wraps naturally

patterns.name: grid
patterns.role: CSS grid container with `--gap`; with `.auto`, lays out a responsive auto-fit grid using `--grid-min`

patterns.name: split
patterns.role: Two-pane horizontal split with items pushed to the ends; container query collapses to vertical at <500px so it adapts to its parent, not the viewport

patterns.name: visually-hidden
patterns.role: Hide content from sighted users but keep it available to screen readers and copy/paste; pair with form labels and icon-only buttons

patterns.name: no-list
patterns.role: Strip default `<ul>`/`<ol>` styling (bullets, spacing) without changing semantics

patterns.name: row
patterns.role: Vertical rhythm between content blocks by default; inside `<form>`/`<fieldset>`, repurposes as a label+input stack (see ADR-0003)

patterns.name: form-actions
patterns.role: Trailing button bar for a form; container-query stacks buttons full-width when the bar gets narrow, so labels don't truncate on small screens

patterns.name: no-margin
patterns.role: Zero out an element's block margin; useful when a heading or paragraph needs to sit flush against its container

patterns.name: self-start
patterns.role: Push a flex/grid child to the cross-axis start; common on form `<label>` next to multi-line controls

patterns.name: full
patterns.role: Force an element to its container's full inline size; useful for buttons inside narrow forms

patterns.name: reset
patterns.role: Reset native button/anchor chrome (background, border, padding, color) so a `<button>` or `<a>` can act as a plain interactive surface without restyling everything by hand

patterns.name: circle
patterns.role: Square element clipped to a circle; configurable via `--size`. Default 40px works for avatars, icon buttons, and dot indicators

patterns.name: layout-card
patterns.role: Auto-fill responsive card grid; columns fit themselves based on `--layout-min-card-width` (default 290px), no breakpoints needed

patterns.name: layout-sidebar
patterns.role: Two-column shell: fixed-width sidebar + flexible main. Sidebar is always the first DOM child for SR order; `.invert` flips visual placement. Collapses to one column under 768px (via container query or media query). Composes with `.drawer` so the sidebar becomes a popover on mobile.

patterns.name: layout-split
patterns.role: Two equal columns side by side; stacks under 768px. `.no-stack` keeps it side-by-side at small widths

patterns.name: layout-three-col
patterns.role: Three equal columns; collapses to two columns under 1024px and one column under 768px

patterns.name: layout-readable
patterns.role: Outer page container that caps width to `--layout-max-width` (default 1200px) and pads the inline edges; the routine wrapper for every page's main content

patterns.name: layout-holy-grail
patterns.role: Three-zone horizontal rail layout (auto | 1fr | auto); use for icon-rail + main + side-rail combinations. Collapses to one column under 768px. See ADR-0010 for the rails repurposing.

patterns.name: section
patterns.role: Block-padded section divider; reach for it to separate hero, features, and footer regions of a page

patterns.name: stack
patterns.role: Vertical flexbox with `--gap` between children; resets default block margins on children so spacing is one consistent knob

patterns.name: cluster
patterns.role: Wrapping horizontal row with small gap (0.5rem default); used for chip rows, tag collections, button groups that should reflow gracefully

patterns.name: carousel
patterns.role: Horizontal scroll-snapping row; each direct child snaps to start. Use for media strips, recommended-item rails, anything that benefits from native trackpad/swipe scrolling

patterns.name: reel
patterns.role: Vertical scroll-snapping column capped at `--reel-height` (default 80vh); use for full-section feeds where each item should snap into view

patterns.name: swipe
patterns.role: Three-pane scroll-snap (left button | content | right button) for swipe-revealable controls; common pattern for inbox row actions and onboarding steps

patterns.name: box
patterns.role: Low-emphasis container for grouping related content; the most neutral surface in the system
patterns.preferOver: custom: bare <div> with manual padding and border

patterns.name: card
patterns.role:
Primary content surface for grouping related media + text; direct `
`/`
`/``/``/`
patterns.example:
Project alpha

Status copy goes here.

Open →

patterns.name: stat-card
patterns.role: A card that surfaces a single metric: `<small>` label + `<strong>` value. Use for dashboards, KPI strips, and summary panels — pick this over a generic `.card` whenever the payload is one number.

patterns.name: feature-card
patterns.role: A larger, hover-lifting card for marketing feature grids; pairs with `.layout-card` to make a row of features

patterns.name: toc
patterns.role: In-page table-of-contents nav; styles a nested `<ol>` into a vertical rail with current-item highlight via `aria-current="true"`
patterns.example:
Contents

  1. Intro
  2. Usage

patterns.name: newsletter
patterns.role: Tinted `--primary` block for newsletter signup CTAs and similar full-bleed callouts; designed to sit inside an article flow

patterns.name: header
patterns.role: Page header bar: logo + nav + actions on a horizontal flex row; styles inline-list `<nav>` automatically. `.sticky` pins it to the top with `--z-overlay`, `.border` adds a subtle hairline.
patterns.example:
…
  • X

patterns.name: callout
patterns.role: Inline emphasis block for notes, warnings, errors, and confirmations; an icon as the first child gets the semantic accent color automatically. `.fill` swaps from hairline to filled tint.
patterns.example: Heads-up: this action can't be undone.

patterns.name: pull-quote
patterns.role: Inline-start-bordered italic quote block for editorial articles; use on `<blockquote>` for semantics
patterns.example: "If you can't measure it, you can't improve it."

patterns.name: table
patterns.role: Wrapper that gives a `<table>` rounded borders and horizontal overflow scrolling without forcing the data layout

patterns.name: form-option-row
patterns.role: Inline checkbox/radio + label row; cursor pointer on the whole row so the affordance reads as one click target

patterns.name: search
patterns.role: Wrap an `<input>` + leading SVG icon so the icon overlays the input's inline-start padding; native search-input affordance without inventing a new control

patterns.name: dropzone
patterns.role: File-upload drop target with dashed border + icon + label; the `<input type="file">` is overlaid full-bleed so the whole area is clickable. Toggle `.dragover` from JS during drag events.

patterns.name: auto-color
patterns.role: Auto-choose black or white text against any `--bg-color`, including transparent ones, by computing effective lightness against the active theme; `.chroma` lets the background tint bleed through the text

patterns.name: surface
patterns.role: Subtle off-`--bg` background for section alternation; overridable via `--surface-bg` for theme accents

patterns.name: breadcrumbs
patterns.role: Wrap a `<nav><ul>` of links into a breadcrumb trail; `--separator` controls the divider character; mark the current page with `aria-current="page"`

patterns.name: pagination
patterns.role: Page-number nav bar: trailing border, gap, and `aria-current="page"` styling on the active link
patterns.example:
Prev
  • 1
  • 2
  • 3
Next

patterns.name: sidebar-nav
patterns.role: Themed vertical nav for sidebars: pressable rows, active highlight via `[aria-current="page"]`, headings via `.sidebar-nav-heading`. Compose with `.minimal` or `.ghost` for variants; `.compact` tightens icon/padding for dense rails.
patterns.example:
Inbox Sent

Folders

Archive

patterns.name: footer
patterns.role: Page footer; container-query collapses nested `.grid` columns under 600px. Underline-on-hover handled automatically for inner links

patterns.name: dropdown
patterns.role: Anchor-positioned dropdown trigger; sibling `.dropdown-menu[popover]` uses CSS `position-anchor` to attach. `.end` aligns the menu to the inline-end edge.
patterns.example: Action

patterns.name: dropdown-header
patterns.role: Small uppercase section heading inside a `.dropdown-menu` to group related items

patterns.name: tooltip
patterns.role: Anchor wrapper for a hover/focus tip. Two usage forms: (1) rich pattern — wrap a trigger and a sibling `.tip` element; (2) standalone — set `aria-label` on a trigger inside `.tooltip` and the pseudo-element renders the text. Tip surface styling is shared between both forms.

patterns.name: avatar
patterns.role: Circular user image or initials. Wrap an `` to crop it to a circle, or use a `

patterns.name: input-group
patterns.role: Visually connect an `<input>` to a trailing `<button>` (search submit, copy-to-clipboard, etc.) without a gap between them. `.stack-mobile` reverts to a stacked layout under 640px.

patterns.name: chat-thread
patterns.role: Outer scroll container for a chat conversation; wraps a sequence of `.chat-row` items with consistent gap

patterns.name: chat-row
patterns.role: A single row in a chat thread; `.self` flips the row's justification to inline-end so the user's own messages align right

patterns.name: chat-message
patterns.role: Width-capped column inside a `.chat-row`; constrains line length to `--chat-message-max-inline` (72ch default)

patterns.name: chat-composer
patterns.role: Bottom-aligned composer bar: input field on the left, send button on the right; designed to dock at the foot of a `.chat-thread`

patterns.name: bubble
patterns.role: Speech-bubble surface (rounded, padded, capped at `--bubble-max-inline`); used by chat messages and any short callout that wants a "spoken" feel

patterns.name: chip
patterns.role: Pressable, pill-shaped filter chip; toggle selected state via `aria-pressed="true"` or `.selected`. Use a `<button>` for keyboard/SR ergonomics

patterns.name: tag
patterns.role: Pill-shaped status badge for metadata; tinted with `--tag-color` (default `--primary`). Compose with `.muted` for a low-key version, swap `--tag-color` for semantic tints (success, warning, error)

patterns.name: list-nav
patterns.role: Vertical stack of card-like nav rows; each row is a button/link with an optional leading icon and trailing description. Use for settings panes, action menus, and primary mobile nav
patterns.example: Profile Edit your account info Billing

patterns.name: tabs
patterns.role: Pure-CSS tabs using `<details>` + grid subgrid for keyboard-driven exclusive selection (no JS). Set `--tab-count` to match the number of tabs. `.boxed` swaps to card-style tabs that connect to the panel below.
patterns.example:
Overview

Panel A

Details

Panel B

Activity

Panel C

patterns.name: app-shell
patterns.role: Top-level mobile-first container: sticky `<header>` + scrolling `<main>` + sticky `<footer>`, with safe-area insets baked in. Reach for it as the page-level wrapper for app-like UIs (not marketing pages).

patterns.name: bottom-nav
patterns.role: Floating bottom tab bar for mobile apps; pinned with safe-area inset, rounded pill shape. Mark the current section with `aria-current="page"` or `.active` for the highlighted state

patterns.name: bottom-sheet
patterns.role: Mobile-style bottom-anchored sheet panel; pseudo-element renders the grip handle, safe-area inset baked into the bottom padding. For full popover/transition behavior compose with `.drawer` (decks)

patterns.name: hide-scrollbar
patterns.role: Hide native scrollbars while keeping overflow scrolling functional; use sparingly — only when the scroll affordance is communicated some other way (snap, fade edges, gesture hints)

patterns.name: momentum-scroll
patterns.role: Enable iOS momentum scrolling + contained overscroll on a scrollable container; pair with `overflow-y: auto`

patterns.name: icon-rail
patterns.role: Narrow vertical column of icon buttons; general-purpose nav rail for agent switchers, tool palettes, workspace shells. Mark the current tool with `aria-current="page"` or `aria-pressed="true"`. Optional `.status` dot per button.

patterns.name: layout-rail
patterns.role: Three-column shell (rail + sub-sidebar + main), optional 4th workbench pane. Container-query-driven mobile collapse at <768px; reveal hidden columns via `.drawer-toggle` + `[popover].drawer` pair (see ADR-0010).

patterns.name: log-card
patterns.role: Compact transcript row with monospace label, optional status slot, and an optional `<pre>` body; use for AI tool calls, deploy logs, build steps, activity feeds
patterns.example:
tool: bash 2.3s 
$ pnpm test

patterns.name: composer
patterns.role: Multi-line text input + inline toolbar; supersedes `.chat-composer` when you need model/tool/attach controls. Also fits comment + post surfaces — anywhere a textarea has adjacent affordances

patterns.name: workbench-panel
patterns.role: Right-aligned IDE-style pane: header bar with inline tabs + scrollable body. Pairs with `.layout-rail.with-workbench` for artifact viewers, properties inspectors, code previews

patterns.name: no-print
patterns.role: Hide an element when the page is printed; only takes effect inside `@media print`. Use on nav, ads, and other screen-only chrome

patternGroups.name: font-size
patternGroups.role: Fluid type-size utilities; each class sets `--fl` to a level on the modular scale (-1 = caption, 0 = body, 6 = display)

patternGroups.name: text-alignment
patternGroups.role: Logical text-alignment helpers; `text-end` flips with writing direction so RTL content lines up correctly

patternGroups.name: aspect-ratios
patternGroups.role: Lock an element to a fixed aspect ratio; use on media containers so images and embeds don't reflow as they load

patternGroups.name: transitions
patternGroups.role: Pre-composed `transition: all <duration> <easing>` utilities built from `--d-*` + `--ease-*`; one-class affordance for elements that animate on hover/active/state

patternGroups.name: gradients
patternGroups.role: Curated, theme-aware gradient utilities — each stop swaps via `light-dark()` so the gradient stays legible across modes. `gradient-text` clips text to a gradient via `background-clip`

patternGroups.name: muted-text
patternGroups.role: De-emphasized text colors; `text-muted` for secondary copy, `text-faint` for tertiary/placeholder

patternGroups.name: safe-area
patternGroups.role: Pad an element by the device's safe-area inset; reach for these on fixed/sticky bars that need to clear the notch / home indicator

tokens.name: --font-sans
tokens.role: Default sans-serif font stack used by body copy and UI; overridden at :root to retheme typography

tokens.name: --box
tokens.role: Composite shadow for the `.box` pattern; soft inset highlight + tiny drop, designed to read as paper rather than elevated surface

tokens.name: --l
tokens.role: Auto-contrast lightness helper used by `auto-color` to flip text between dark and light against `currentColor`; do not set directly

tokenGroups.name: fluid-viewport-bounds
tokenGroups.role: Inclusive viewport range (in raw px) that bounds fluid type and spacing calculations

tokenGroups.name: fluid-type-config
tokenGroups.role: Anchor values for the fluid type scale; `--fl` levels interpolate between these min/max ratios across the viewport range

tokenGroups.name: vertical-spacing
tokenGroups.role: Vertical rhythm scale; use for `gap` on stacks and for `margin-block` between content blocks
tokenGroups.scale: xs, s, base, m, l, xl, xxl, xxxl

tokenGroups.name: padding
tokenGroups.role: Inset spacing scale; use for component `padding` and inside-edge gaps
tokenGroups.scale: xs, s, m, l, xl, xxl, xxxl

tokenGroups.name: border-radius
tokenGroups.role: Border radius scale; pick by component size, not personal preference
tokenGroups.scale: xs, s, m, l, xl, xxl

tokenGroups.name: line-height
tokenGroups.role: Line-height scale; `--lh` is body default, smaller for dense UI, larger for long-form reading

tokenGroups.name: font-weight
tokenGroups.role: Type weight tokens; align with the variable-font axis points the project ships

tokenGroups.name: heading-letter-spacing
tokenGroups.role: Negative tracking for large headings (H1–H3) so big type doesn't visually drift apart

tokenGroups.name: base-colors
tokenGroups.role: Canonical hue anchors in OKLCH; every per-color scale (`--<color>-1..9`, `--<color>-opaque-*`) derives from these

tokenGroups.name: color-yellow
tokenGroups.role: Yellow scale (alpha-derived steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`; pick a scale step by needed contrast, not by mood

tokenGroups.name: color-amber
tokenGroups.role: Amber scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-orange
tokenGroups.role: Orange scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-red
tokenGroups.role: Red scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; used for `--error` and destructive surfaces

tokenGroups.name: color-pink
tokenGroups.role: Pink scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-purple
tokenGroups.role: Purple scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; distinct from `--purple-deep-*`

tokenGroups.name: color-purple-deep
tokenGroups.role: Deeper purple than `--purple` for high-contrast UI; includes base hue plus alpha + opaque scales

tokenGroups.name: color-indigo
tokenGroups.role: Indigo scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-green
tokenGroups.role: Green scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; used for `--success` and confirmation surfaces

tokenGroups.name: color-lime
tokenGroups.role: Lime scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-highlighter
tokenGroups.role: Highlighter scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; loud yellow-green for emphasis and selection

tokenGroups.name: color-brown
tokenGroups.role: Brown scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-teal
tokenGroups.role: Teal scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants pre-mixed against `--bg`/`--fg`

tokenGroups.name: color-blue
tokenGroups.role: Blue scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; default source for `--primary`

tokenGroups.name: color-gray
tokenGroups.role: Neutral gray scale plus the `--gray` and `--slate` hue anchors; backbone for UI chrome and disabled states

tokenGroups.name: color-slate
tokenGroups.role: Slate scale (alpha steps 1–9) plus opaque variants; cooler neutral alternative to `--gray-*`

tokenGroups.name: color-white
tokenGroups.role: Pure white anchor plus alpha + opaque scales; static across light/dark theme (does NOT swap)

tokenGroups.name: color-black
tokenGroups.role: Pure black anchor plus alpha + opaque scales; static across light/dark theme (does NOT swap)

tokenGroups.name: fg-bg-literals
tokenGroups.role: Literal light/dark text and surface anchors used by `light-dark()` to compute `--fg` and `--bg`

tokenGroups.name: easing
tokenGroups.role: Easing curves expressed with `linear()` for natural motion; choose smooth for UI, bounce for affordances, emphasized for hero transitions

tokenGroups.name: duration
tokenGroups.role: Semantic motion duration scale; reach for these instead of raw seconds so motion feels consistent across components. See docs/adr/0008.

tokenGroups.name: z-index
tokenGroups.role: Stacking-context vocabulary; pick by purpose (overlay, sticky, modal, toast), not by raw number. See docs/adr/0008.
tokenGroups.scale: base, raised, overlay, sticky, modal, toast

tokenGroups.name: color-fg
tokenGroups.role: Foreground (text + icon) semantic color; resolves to light or dark via `light-dark()`. Use `--fg-N` for muted/secondary text and borders, `--fg-opaque-N` when transparency would composite poorly.

tokenGroups.name: color-bg
tokenGroups.role: Background (surface) semantic color; resolves to light or dark via `light-dark()`. Use `--bg-N` for layered surface depth, `--bg-opaque-N` when stacking against transparency-sensitive content.

tokenGroups.name: status-colors
tokenGroups.role: Semantic role colors that derive scales (`--primary-*`, `--error-*`, etc.); rethemes follow when you reassign these

tokenGroups.name: color-primary
tokenGroups.role: Alpha (1–9) and opaque scales derived from `--primary`; reach for these on accent surfaces and CTAs

tokenGroups.name: color-error
tokenGroups.role: Alpha (1–9) and opaque scales derived from `--error`; use for destructive actions and validation surfaces

tokenGroups.name: color-warning
tokenGroups.role: Alpha (1–9) and opaque scales derived from `--warning`; use for caution states that aren't destructive

tokenGroups.name: color-success
tokenGroups.role: Alpha (1–9) and opaque scales derived from `--success`; use for confirmation and positive feedback surfaces

tokenGroups.name: shadows
tokenGroups.role: Elevation shadow ramp; pick by stacking order (`1` = subtle inline, `6` = drawn-up overlay), each step uses `light-dark()` so the shadow stays visible in dark mode

tokenGroups.name: borders
tokenGroups.role: Pre-composed `border` shorthand tokens (style + width + `--fg`-derived color); set `border: var(--border-2)` instead of stacking width and color manually

tokenGroups.name: focus-ring
tokenGroups.role: Outline + offset values for `:focus-visible`; consumed by every interactive surface so the focus indicator stays consistent (use `--focus-ring-offset-inset` when the element clips its outline)
